Name: Clefable
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable’s type is still Colorless.)
All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent’s next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Electrode
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon isn’t Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own).
Flavor-Text: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Discard 1 [R] Energy card attached to Flareon in order to use this attack.
Flavor-Text: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It accumulates negative ions from the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother’s protective pouch until it is three years old.

Name: Mr. Mime
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Whenever an attack (including your own) does 30 or more damage to Mr. Mime (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power can’t be used if Mr. Mime is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If interrupted while miming, it will slap around the enemy with its broad hands.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.
Flavor-Text: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing its victim around and toss it hard.

Name: Scyther
Effects: During your next turn, Scyther’s Slash attack’s base damage is 60 instead of 30.
Flavor-Text: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of it.

Name: Snorlax
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Snorlax can’t become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. This power can’t be used if Snorlax is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Vaporeon but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. Extra [W] Energy after the 2nd doesn’t count.
Flavor-Text: Lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin that is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Venomoth
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may change the type of Venomoth to the type of any other Pokémon in play other than Colorless. This power can’t be used if Venomoth is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

Name: Victreebel
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Vileplume
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. Vileplume is now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable’s type is still Colorless.)
All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent’s next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Effects: If the Defending Pokémon isn’t Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own).
Flavor-Text: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

Effects: During your next turn, Scyther’s Slash attack’s base damage is 60 instead of 30.

Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Snorlax can’t become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. This power can’t be used if Snorlax is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Vaporeon but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. Extra [W] Energy after the 2nd doesn’t count.
Flavor-Text: Lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin that is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Venomoth
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may change the type of Venomoth to the type of any other Pokémon in play other than Colorless. This power can’t be used if Venomoth is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

Name: Victreebel
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Vileplume
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. Vileplume is now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10).
Flavor-Text: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

Name: Dodrio
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: As long as Dodrio is Benched, pay [C] less to retreat your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dodrio.
Flavor-Text: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Fearow.
Flavor-Text: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

Name: Gloom
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Both the Defending Pokémon and Gloom are now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn’t drool; it is a nectar that is used to attract prey.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon’s. It leaves a stinging sensation when it licks enemies.

Name: Marowak
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Search your deck for a [F] Basic Pokémon card and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)
Flavor-Text: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to K.O. targets.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The female’s horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

Name: Parasect
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

Name: Persian
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Persian during your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Flavor-Text: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If tails, Primeape is now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: Always furious and tenacious to boot. It will not abandon chasing its quarry until its quarry is caught.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Rapidash.
Flavor-Text: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is knocked out.)
Flavor-Text: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

Name: Seaking
Flavor-Text: In the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming powerfully up rivers and creeks.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

Name: Weepinbell
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spits out poisonpowder to immobilize the enemy, and then finishes the enemy with a spray of acid.

Name: Bellsprout
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Bellsprout and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)
Flavor-Text: A carniverous Pokémon that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

Name: Cubone
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Cubone during your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cubone.
Flavor-Text: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this Pokémon’s real face.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack Eevee during your opponent’s next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from elemental stones.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Exeggcute.
Flavor-Text: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

Name: Goldeen
Flavor-Text: Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname “Water Queen.”

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

Name: Mankey
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at one of the following: the top card of either player’s deck, a random card from your opponent’s hand, or one of either player’s Prizes. This power can’t be used if Mankey is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Nidoran Male or Nidoran Female and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)
Flavor-Text: Although small, its venomous barbs make this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

Name: Oddish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Oddish and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)
Flavor-Text: During the day, it keeps its face buried in the ground. At night, it wanders around sowing its seeds.

Name: Paras
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack Rhyhorn during your opponent’s next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Flavor-Text: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

Name: Spearow
Effects: If Spearow was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Spearow to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speeds to stay airborne.

Name: Venonat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Remove a number of damage counters from Venonat equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Lives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats insects. It is attracted by light at night.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for any Basic Pokémon or Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
